title: Venedig, San Zanipolo (Basilica dei Santi Giovanni e Paolo), Grabmal des Dogen Alvise Mocenigo I. und Gattin (tomb of Doge Alvise Mocenigo I. and spouse)
tags: venice venezia venedig camposanzanipolo sestierecastello basilicadeisantigiovanniepaolo sanzanipolo basilicaofsaintsjohnandpaul grabmaldesdogenalvisemocenigoi monumentofunebrealdogealviseimocenigo funeralmonumentofdogealvisemocenigoi
description: Alvise Mocenigo I. 1(507 - 1577) war von 1570 - 1577 Doge von Venedig. Das Doppelgrab für ihn und seine Frau Loredana  an der Westwand über dem Portal von San Zanipolo wurde von Giovanni Girolamo Grapiglia  und Francesco Contino entworfen und erbaut. Im zweiten Geschoss des Grabmals, das eher einer Kirchenfassade als einem Grabmal ähnelt, befinden sich die beiden Sarkophage mit den Liegefiguren zu Seiten des segnenden Christus.

title: Santi Giovanni e Paolo Memorial II
tags: city travel venice vacation italy sculpture holiday heritage history church statue architecture italian worship europe italia religion culture eu unescoworldheritagesite unesco worldheritagesite chiesa venetian christianity catholicism venezia europeanunion venetia romancatholic historiccity citta southerneurope gothicarchitecture centrostorico veneto thelagoon gothicchurch worldheritagelist northernitaly romancatholicchurch italianheritage theveneto venetianlagoon santigiovanniepaolo italianculture italianhistory sanzanipolo cittàdivenezia communedivenezia veneziaeuganea veniceanditslagoons
description:
One of the memorials to the Doges inside the church of Santi Giovanni e Paolo in Venice.

Santi Giovanni e Paolo (San Zanipolo in the Venetian dialect) is a large Gothic church in the Castello sestiere, built from red brick and containing memorials and tombs of many of the city’s Doges.

A total of 27 Doges are buried within the church, with their memorials a variety of sculpting styles – baroque, Gothic and more – and in contrast to the plain, if colossal, brick exterior.

The majority of the memorials belong to members of the Mocenigo family, one of the most prominent of the Venetian Republic, though it was built after Doge Giacomo Tiepolo donated the land to the Dominicans in 1246. The current building dates to the 14th century, the original having been knocked down.

tags: city travel venice vacation italy holiday tower heritage history church architecture italian worship europe italia religion culture eu belltower unescoworldheritagesite unesco worldheritagesite chiesa campanile venetian christianity catholicism venezia europeanunion venetia romancatholic historiccity citta southerneurope gothicarchitecture centrostorico veneto thelagoon gothicchurch worldheritagelist northernitaly romancatholicchurch italianheritage theveneto venetianlagoon santigiovanniepaolo italianculture italianhistory sanzanipolo bartolomeobon cittàdivenezia communedivenezia veneziaeuganea
description:
The view of the church Santi Giovanni e Paolo in venice, seen from the top of the Campanile in St Mark's Square.

Santi Giovanni e Paolo (San Zanipolo in the Venetian dialect) is a large Gothic church in the Castello sestiere, built from red brick and containing memorials and tombs of many of the city’s Doges.

A total of 27 Doges are buried within the church, with their memorials a variety of sculpting styles – baroque, Gothic and more – and in contrast to the plain, if colossal, brick exterior.

The majority of the memorials belong to members of the Mocenigo family, one of the most prominent of the Venetian Republic, though it was built after Doge Giacomo Tiepolo donated the land to the Dominicans in 1246. The current building dates to the 14th century, the original having been knocked down.

title: San Zanipolo and the campanile of Santa Maria Formosa, Venice
tags: venice italy belltower campanile ssgiovanniepaolo santamariaformosa sanzanipolo
description:
San Zanipolo is the name in Venetian dialect of the Basilica di San Giovanni e Paolo, a church in Venice, Italy.  Built in brick in the Italian Gothic style, its construction began in 1333 and was completed in 1430.  It is the principal Dominican church in Venice.  After the 15th century, the burial service of every Doge was held here, and 25 Doges are buried in the church.

In front of it can be seen the white marble campanile (and, to its right, the dome) of the church of Santa Maria Formosa.  This was built in 1492 by Renaissance architect Mauro Codussi.

This photo is taken from the roof of St Mark's Basilica, looking north-east.

title: Venedig, Campo San Zanipolo, Condottiere Bartolomeo Colleoni von Andrea del Verrocchio
tags: venedig venezia venice sestierecastello camposanzanipolo condottierebartolomeocolleoni andreadelverrocchio reiterstandbild equestrianstatue publicart kunstimöffentlichenraum
description:
Auf dem Campo San Zanipolo  steht das von Andrea del Verrocchio geschaffene Reiterstandbild des Condottiere Bartolomeo Colleoni (1400 - 1475), dem Venedig die Kriegsführung auf der Terraferma  ab 1448 anvertraute. Er vermachte Venedig 100.000 Golddukaten mit der Auflage, ihm ein Reiterstandbild vor San Marco  zu errichten. 5 Jahre diskutierte man, wie man die Aufstellung auf der Piazza San Marco vermeiden könne. Da das Testament nicht ausdrücklich von der Kirche San Marco sprach, wurde entschieden, das Denkmal vor der Scuola San Marco  zu errichten, so dass das Erbe durch diesen Kniff angetreten werden konnte. 

Andrea del Verrocchio (Florenz 1435/36 - Venedig 1488) wurde als Andrea di Michele Cione in Florenz geboren. Er lernte bei dem Goldschmied Giuliano del Verrocchio, dessen Namen er annahm, und war nach seiner Lehrzeit als Bronzebildner, Goldschmied, Bildhauer und Maler tätig.

title: Venedig, San Zanipolo (Basilica dei Santi Giovanni e Paolo), Grabmal des Dogen Pietro Mocenigo von Pietro Lombardo (tomb of Doge Pietro Mocenigo by Pietro Lombardo)
tags: venice venezia venedig pietrolombardo camposanzanipolo sestierecastello basilicadeisantigiovanniepaolo sanzanipolo basilicaofsaintsjohnandpaul monumentofunebrealdogepietromocenigo grabmaldesdogenpietromocenigo funeralmonumentofdogepietromocenigo
description:
Pietro Mocenigo (ca. 1405 - 1476) war von 1474 - 1476 Doge und einer der erfolgreichsten Kriegshelden Venedigs. Das von Pietro Lombardo 1481 zusammen mit seinen Söhnen Tullio und Antonio gestaltete Grabmonument zeigt erstmals in Venedig das römische Triumphbogenmotiv  und würdigt vor allem die persönlichen Erfolge des verstorbenen Dogen, während christliche Motive vernachlässigt werden. Der Sockel des Grabmonuments aus weißem Carrara-Marmor ist  mit Reliefs geschmückt, die Taten antiker Helden darstellen. Unter dem zentralen Bogen steht der Doge Pietro Mocenigo in der Prunkrüstung eines "Capitano da Mar" in einer Pose ähnlich dem auferstandenen Christus auf einem aufwändigen Sarkophag, den drei Krieger wie im Triumph präsentieren.

.

title: Venedig, San Zanipolo (Basilica dei Santi Giovanni e Paolo), Grabmal des Dogen Pietro Mocenigo von Pietro Lombardo (tomb of Doge Pietro Mocenigo by Pietro Lombardo)
tags: venice venezia venedig pietrolombardo camposanzanipolo sestierecastello basilicadeisantigiovanniepaolo sanzanipolo basilicaofsaintsjohnandpaul monumentofunebrealdogepietromocenigo grabmaldesdogenpietromocenigo funeralmonumentofdogepietromocenigo
description:
Pietro Mocenigo (ca. 1405 - 1476) war von 1474 - 1476 Doge und einer der eerfolgreichsten Kriegshelden Venedigs. Das von Pietro Lombardo 1481  zusammen mit seinen Söhnen Tullio und Antonio gestaltete Grabmonument zeigt erstmals in Venedig das römische Triumphbogenmotiv  und würdigt vor allem die persönlichen Erfolge des verstorbenen Dogen, während christliche Motive vernachlässigt werden. Der Sockel des Grabmonuments aus weißem Carrara-Marmor ist  mit Reliefs geschmückt, die Taten antiker Helden dargestellen. Unter dem zentralen Bogen steht der Doge Pietro Mocenigo in der Prunkrüstung eines "Capitano da Mar" in einer Pose ähnlich dem auferstandenen Christus auf einem aufwändigen Sarkophag, den drei Krieger wie im Triumph präsentieren.

description: Hyazinthus von Polen, Ordensmann, Glaubensbote * um 1183 in Groß-Stein, heute Kamień Śląski bei Opole in Polen † 15. August 1257 in Krakau in Polen Jacek Odrowąż stammte aus dem schlesisch-polnischen Adelsgeschlecht der Odrowąż und war ein Verwandter von Bischof Ivo von Krakau. Er studierte in Krakau, Prag und Bologna, wo er zum Doktor für kanonisches Recht und Theologie promoviert wurde. Er wurde - zusammen mit seinem Bruder Ceslaus - Kanoniker des Domkapitels in Krakau und trat 1218 in Rom in den Dominikanerorden ein. Dominikus schickte ihn 1219 nach Polen zurück, auf dem Weg gründete er 1221 in Friesach in Kärnten zusammen mit Hermann dem Deutschen ein erstes Dominikanerkloster. Er predigte unaufhörlich, gründete Klöster in Polen, Schlesien, Pommern, Preußen und Russland, so 1222 in Krakau, 1226 in Wrocław, 1227 in Danzig, und 1229 in Kiew, wo er bis 1233 lebte. Mit Marienstatue und Ziborium (das Ziborium, lateinisch für „Trinkbecher”, ist ein in der katholischen und den orthodoxen Kirchen benutztes Gefäß zur Aufbewahrung der geweihten Hostien, benutzt vor allem bei Haus- und Krankenkommunion) soll Hyazinthus dort trockenen Fußes über den Dnjepr geschritten und so den die Stadt belagernden Tataren entkommen sein. Anschließend missionierte er in Pommern und bei den preußischen Pomesaniern und gründete die Klöster in Kamień Śląski und Elbing - dem heutigen Elbląg und schuf die Ordensprovinz Polonia, die sich von Preußen bis nach Russland erstreckte. Hyazinth war auch in Litauen, Dänemark, Schweden und Russland als Missionar aktiv und trat auch in Preußen als Missionsprediger auf, wo der Deutsche Orden seine Bemühungen aber durch die eigene Mission mit Waffengewalt verunmöglichte. Heilungen, Totenerweckungen und zahlreiche Wunder werden ihm zugeschrieben. Die bedeutendste Quelle für Hyazinths Leben ist die nach 1352 verfasste Lebensgeschichte. Quelle: www.heiligenlexikon.de/

title: vittor pisani
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice

Vittor Pisani d. 1380 attributed to Bonino da Campione.
Restored in 1921.

Born 1324.
He was commander of the Venetian fleet in 1378 during the war against the Geoese, who he defeated off Capo d'Anzio.
He was responsible for the capture of Kotor, Sibenik and Rab.
1379 the Genoese fleet turned and defeated Pisani. When he returned to Venice he was put in prison, however, the people of Venice demanded his liberation when Venice itself was threatened after the capture of Chioggia.
Given a second chance he was able to recaapture Chioggia with the Venetian fleet and held them off until peace agreements were reached.
1380on his way to Manfredonia with a fleet of provision ships he died.
It is believed that his palace is now the Hotel Saturnia

title: valier monument
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice

Valier funeral monument, is the entrance to the Chapel of Peace. it represents Doge Bertucci (1658), the Doge Silvestro (1700) and his wife Elisabetta Guerrini (1708), created by Andrea Tirali, based on the drawings by  Antonio Gaspari. it also features reliefs of virtue.

Doge Bertuccio Valiero
Born in Venice in July 1596 the son of Silvestro Valiero and Bianca Priuli.
He was a cultured man and married Benedetta Pisani, together having many children, including his son silvestro who would later serve as Doge and appears on the monument.
Served as Podesta of Venices towns and as Venetian Ambassador to the Vatican durinf the pontificate of Pope Alexander VII.
1656 during the war with the Ottoman empire he was considered as a candidate. He lost the election, but Francesco Cornaro died only 19 days later, meaning a second election was held and he was unanimously elected.
He attempted to to bring the ottoman war to an end but treaties were rejected and the fleet only had limited success at the seige o Candia.
Desprate for money in 1655 the Jesuits were allowed to return to Venice on agreement of payment to the state.
1658, after suffering from ill health for many years he finally died in March 1658,

Silvestro Valiero
Was born in venice in March 1630.
Son of the above, he married Elisabetta when he was 19.
He became procurator by buying his way into the office. He was interested in the diplomatic affairs of the state.
He was considered a good speaker, loved the good life but was also generous to the poor and gained the peoples affection.
He was elected as Doge in 1694 after the republic decided they wanted someone who was not ambious beacause of the poor economic state through war.
The council had done away with the grand procession reserved for the Dogaressa, but they were persuaded to reinstate it for Elisabetta. in March 1694 she appeared clothed in golden robe finished with sable, a white veil and the ducal drown, finished of by grand jewels including a diamond cross on her chest.
He died in july 1700 at war with his own family and Venice on the verge of bankrupcy.

title: the rosary chapel
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice

The Rosary chapel was built to give thanks to the Virgin of the Roary. The work was assigned to Alessandro Vittoria from Trento who died in 1608  before the work was completed.
The mutilated torsos are by Vittoria representing St Thomas and Rose from Lima.
the original interior was lost during the fire of 1867, but was restored to its present glory in the later 1920s.

title: the magdalene and salomoni tomb
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice

The chapel of the Name of God, Now the the Chapel of the Beatified Giacomo Salomoni. It was erected in the mid 14th century in honour of Ludovico de Tolosa.
The altar piece is La Maddalena and St louis of Toulouse at the foot of the Crucifix (1650) in rememberance of the first dedication of the chapel. it is considered to be the first work by Pietro Liberi.
Placed at the altar is the remains of Giacomo Salomoni 1231-1314, a Dominican son of the adjacent convent. It was believed his miracle was the ability to invoke against tumours.

title: the count of pitigliano nicolo orsini 1509
tags: sculpture horse monument war mercenary veniceitalychurchcheisa
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice

Niccolo di Pitigliano, Count of Orsini, mounted upon his war horse from Colleoni and flanked by Faith and Prudence. (1442-1510)
Born in Pitigliano to Aldobrandino II, count of Pitigliano and his wife Bartolomea.
coming from a famous family he made his name as a mercenary, taking contracts from Florence, Siena, Naples and the Pope amongst others. 
1495 until he died he was governatore Generale delle Milizie Veneziane.
1509 opposed an attack by the frnech King lousie XII, but due to in house fighting between Pitigliano and Alviano failed to unite their forces and reseulted in Alvasinos army being slaughtered in May. Pitigliao's forces rdeserted and he was forced to retreat.
The French has won and proceeded to occupy vas amounts of Venetian land.
By November he had regrouped and easily defeated the army that he faced, although he was later driven back to Padua  after the advance at Po River.

He was married twice. Firstly in 1467 to Elena dei Conti Montalcino and secondly to Guglielmina, a woman of common birth.

title: st dominic chapel
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice

The Chapel of st Dominic was begun in 1690 to replace the idea of originaly concieved in 1348 by Andrea Tirali following the designs of Gaspari.
The base reliefs of 1717 are by uiseppe Mazza of Bologna, illustrating the life of the Saint.
The ceiling iscarved by Pietro Bernardoni in gold leaf with a huge canvas by Giambattista Piazzetta (1725) depicting The Apostatises of St. dominic, transported to the heavens and the waiting Trinity and Virgin, whilst the four corners depict Religion, Meekness, Fortitude and Justice (1727).

title: pompeo giustiniani 1616
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice

Pompeo Giustiniani by Francesco Terilli da Feltre.
pompeo was born in 1569 in Ajaccio.
He was 14 when he began his military career.
1602 during the Seige of ostend he was promoted to Mastro di Campo.
He later wrote a text "Delle guerre di Fiandra" based on his experiences and had it publishedin 1609.
1613 he entered the Venetian army, but argued with the other officers at Uskok.
1616 he beseiged Gradisca but had to withdraw because the Habsburg forces were too strong. 
10 October 1616 he was killed by the enemy army at Lucinico.

title: marcantonio bragadin 1571
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice, Italy

The Monument to Marcantonio Bragadin attributed to Vincezo Scamozzi with a fresco above depicting the scene of Martyrdom of Mercenary Leader which is attributed to Cosimo Da Castelfranco.
Marcantonio Bragadin was born 21 April 1523.
He was a lawyer in 1543, but decided to pursue a career in the navy.
1560 he was made a city magistrate, and in 1566 he was made a galley governor.
1569 he was elected as Captain of the Kingdom of Cyprus and moved to Famagusta which at that time was a rich and wealthy port. He assumed civil governorship over the whole island. He worked hard to fortify Famagusta. The Turks landed in Cyprus on july 3, 1570. they beseiged Famagusta in September
He had joined the Fanti da Mar Corps of the Republic of Venice. In 1569 he was appointed Captain General of Famagusta in Cyprus.
31 July 1571 after a long and brutal seige he agreed to negotiate terms of surrender.
Terms were excepted and the Ottoman leaders allowed all Christians to leave the city in peace under their own flag. This happened for four days with no problems, but on the fifth day during the presentation of the city to the Ottomans, their General Mustafa started to behave strangely accusing Bragdin of murdering Turkish prisoners. Mustafa then pulled out his knife and cut off Bragadin's right ear. He ordered his guards to cut off the other ear and his nose. The order was then given that any remaining Christians were slaughtered.
Bragadin was thrown in prison for two weeks with feastering wounds before he was dragged round the city walls with sacks of stones and earth on his back. when they had finished humiliating him they tied him naked to a post in the centre of the square and was flayed alive.
they abused his quartered body further before sending what remained as a gift to the Sultan selim II of Constantinople, where it was kept in the treasury. It was captured by A Ventian officer, Girolamo Polidori in 1580. The skin was returned to the church and preserved. in Venice he was considered a Martyr.

title: edward duke of windsor 1574
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice, Italy

The monument to edward, Duke of Windsor attributed to Alessandro Vittoria.

Sir Edward Windsor, 2rd Baron Winsor was born in 1532.
He was married to Katherine de Vere, the older half sister of Edward de Vere, 17th Earl of Oxford.
On 2 October 1553, the day after the coronation of Queen Mary, he was appointed a Knight of the Carpet.
In 1557 he fought in the battle of St Quentin during the Italian wars, under the english forces of Frances Russell, 2nd earl of Bedford.
1566 he was visited at the family home by Queen Elizabeth, but because he was a firm Roman Catholic he spent many years living in europe.
He died in Venice in 24 January 1574.

title: doge tommaso mocenigo 1423
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice, Italy

Monument to Doge Tommaso Mocenigo by Pietro Di Niccolo Lamberti and Giovanni Di Martino da Fiesole.
Born in 1343, he was in command of the crusading fleet in the expedition to Nicopolis in 1396.
1378-81 he won the battle against the Genoese during the war of Chioggia.
He was Venetian ambassador at Cremona, during which posting he was elected doge in 141. He was thus forced to escape fearing he would be held prisoner by Gabrino Fondolo, tyrant of Cremona.
His fleet would later defeat the Turks at Gallipoli.
He reconstructed the ducal palace and started the new library.
He died after a long illness in 1423.

title: doge sebastiano venier 1578
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice, Italy

Monument to the Mercenary Sebastiano Venier by Antonio Dal Zotto.
Sebastiano Venier was borrn 1496 the son of Moise Venier and Elena Dona.
He worked as a lawyer from an early age and later became an administrator for the Venice Government.
1570 he was procurator and later Capitano generale de mar of the Venetian fleet.
He was commander at the Battle of Lepanto in 1571 in which the Christians defeated the Turks.
1577 at the age of 81 he was elected doge.
He was married to Cecilia Contarini with whom he had a daughter, Elena, and two sons.
He died in March 1578 allegedly of  a broken heart after a fire heavily damaged the Doges Palace.

1475 he defeated the Turks at the siege of Scutari, where he contracted an illness and died in 1476.

He was born in 1392 and was a statesman before becoming Doge in October 1457. He was responsible for passing a law that limited the poeer of the Council of Ten.
He died 7 May 1462

title: doge nicolo marcello
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice, Italy

Monument to doge Nicolo Marcello attributed to Pietro Lombardo, displayed with the four virtues and the eternal Father.
Nicolo was born in 1399, he was a trader with the Orient before becoming a state figure including Provost tot he Council of Ten.
He married twice, firstly to Bianca Barbarigo and then, secondly, to contarina Contarni.
1473 he was elected Doge during which time he devoted his time to reorganizing the state finances of Venice.
He introduced a new silver coin called Marcello.
He died in December 1474

description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice, Italy

The double monument to Michele steno and Alvise trevisan.
Michele Steno was born in 1331.
As a young man he was involved in an act of vandalism against the then doge by profaining his wife, and was nearly executed for the crime.
He later served as Proveditor of Venice, and a capable diplomate.
1400 he was elected Doge.
He died in December 1413

Alvise Trevisan was a scholar who died in 1528.

title: doge michele morosini
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice, Italy

The monument of Doge Michele Morosini. The mosiac depicts the Doge and his wife cristina Bondumier kneeling and prayinf before Christ on the Cross.
Michele Morosini was born in 1308 into one of the wealthiest families in Venice.
He was elected Doge in June 1382 but died of plague in Ocotber the same year.

Died in 1368

description:

Leonardo Loredan was Doge 1501-21.

title: doge jacopo tiepolo and lorenzo
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice

Doge Jacepo Tiepolo (1229-1249)  and his son Lorenzo (1268-75).

The decoration on the tomb alludes to a dream had by the Doge which lead to the land being donated for the building of the church.

Doge Jacopo before he was elected Doge he served as first Duke of Crete and Podesta in Constantinopole (1218-20 / 1224-27).
He went for election as Doge in 1229 against his rival Marino Dandolo. The election was tight, the original count being 20 votes each. It would later lead to a rift between the two families.
1242 he proclaimed statuto, the Codification of Venetian Civil Laws.
1240s The Great Mendicant; where the orders of the Dominican and Fransiscan orders were granted land on which to build the two largest churches in venice: the Sant Giovanni e Paolo and Basilica di santa Maria Gloriosa dei Frari.
1249 he abdicated as Doge and retired to the family home where he died a couple of months later on the 19 July 1249.

Lorenzo was the son of Jacopo and his second wife Marguerite the daughter of the king of Romania.
1257 - commanded during the War of Saint Sabas with the Genoes who he defeated at Acre.
Served as Podesta of Fano.
1268 elected Doge with 25 votes from 41.
He was generally loved by the people but received much hostillity from the nobles because of the way he treated hus son.
1273 Marco Polo set off for his exploration of china under the Dogal blessing.
15 Aug 1275 he died

title: doge giovanni dolfin 1361
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice, Italy

Monument to Doge Dolfin by Andrea da San Felice. The front panels depict The Adoration of the Magi, the doges and their wives adoring Christ on the throne and the passing away of the Virgin Mary. The end figures represent the annunciation.
Born into a wealthy family he was elected Doge in August 1356. 
during his rule there was much unrest in the Venetian colonies.
He died in July 1361

title: doge giovani mocenigo detail
description:
Basilica Giovanni e Paolo

Monument to doge Giovanni Mocenigo, attributed to Tulio Lombardo in 1552. The carvings of the base relief is attributed to Antonio Lombardo an illustrate the Baptism of Christ and St Mark Baptising Aniano and his family.
Giovanni Ser di Mocenigo Jr was born in 1409.
He fought at sea against the Ottoman Sultan Mehmed II and also against Ercole I d'este, Duke of Ferrera, from whom he captured Rovigo and Polesine.
It is speculated that he was killed by poisoning. in 1485.
He was Doge from 1478.

title: doge antonio vernier 1400
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice, Italy

The monument to Doge Antonio Vernier by Dalle Masegne, upon which five niches hold the virtues. To the side is the Saint Dominic and Anthony the Abbot. Above is the virgin Mary flanked by St Peter and Paul.
Antonio Venier was born in 1330.
He married Agnes da Mosto.
1380 he was in the army at the rank of Superintendent in the Fortress of Tenedos.
1381 became Captain of Crete.
1382 he was elected Doge.
His rule was not an easy one annotated by years of floods and plagues, plus brutal wars abroad. He also had problems with his son Alvise which lead to mockery of the family through his adulterous ways. He was imprisoned and fell ill there, but his father did not release him.
He died in November 1400

title: doge andrea vendramin
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice, Italy

Monument to Doge Andrea Vendramin by Tullio Lombardo 1493, surrounded by the seven virtues.

Andrea Vendramin was born in 1393 to Maria Michiel.
He served as Venetian Procurator in Rome, and also had a joint shipping venture with his brother inporting food from Alexandria.
He was elected doge in 1476 which caused so much ill feeling that Antonio Feleto was imprisoned and then bannished for calling him a cheesemonger Doge.
He married Regina Gradenigo had six daughters.
He died of plague in May 1478

title: doge alvise mocenigo
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice, Italy

The monument to Doge Alvise Mocenigo and his wife Loredana Marcello.
The work was begun in 1577 by Girolamo Grapiglia and was finished in 1646 by Francesco Contin. the monument is so huge that it encompasses the enitre facade of the entrance and includes the monument to Bartolomeo Bragadin from 1507.
Alvise I Mocenigo was born 26 October 1507.
He was a diplomat for Venice at the Court of Emperor Charles V in 1545 and to Pope Paul IV in 1557 before returning to the imperial court in 1567.
In 1570 he was elected as Doge. during his rule Venice lost the fortresses of Nicosia and Famagusta. 
He died 4 June 1577.

title: altar st vincent ferrer
description:
Basilica Giovani e Paolo, Venice, Italy

The Altar of St Vincent Ferrer was orginally constructedi in 1454, but the current one dates from 1523. It contains the remians of Brother tommaso Caffarini who died in 1434 and was the founder of the Dominican Tertiary Order. it incorpurates the first works of Giovanni Bellini, and depicts the Announcing Angel, Chrsit deceased held up by two angels, Madonna of the Annunciation. the central section shows St. Christopher, st Vincent ferrer and St Sebastian. The five miracles of the saint are depicted in the lower panels.

title: A Church for Heather
tags: november venice italy nikon 2011 d90 basilicadeisantigiovanniepaolo
description:
The title kind of sounds like a band name doesn't it? In this case though, it's a dedication to a long-time Flickr pal and fan of ecclesiastic architecture, hmc_chic. 

I haven't posted a church shot in a while. I think it has something to do with missing my old neighborhood church, the Bern Münster. It's a tough one to top. 

Anyway, this is the Basilica Dei Santi Giovanni E Paolo in Venice. More specifically, this is the Chapella di San Domenico, one of seven chapels in this magnificent 14th century cathedral. The whole place was such a hidden gem. It's off the beaten path, away from the bulk of the tourists and it costs two euros to enter whereas the main tourist attraction, the Basilica di San Marco is free. Most tourists who entered this church stopped at the pay booth, took a few snaps and walked out. Thus, for a mere two euros, I almost had the place to myself and on a Saturday afternoon yet. It was great!

 Sadly, I had to shoot off my camera bag as I didn't have my tripod that day. You see, the Italians are vehemently against photography in most of their tourist trap locations so after a day of carrying it and not being able to even shoot let alone use the tripod, I left it in the hotel room.  I think I could have used one here though as there were no signs at all. Next time I go I'll be sure to pack it to this place. 

So, making a short story long, there you have it, a church for Heather. =)

Especially if it's home to beautiful gems like this one. This is going on my top list, Michael. And it's going to be a close call between this and Bern. I absolutely love the detail you've captured, from the ethreal quality of the fresco in the dome to the reliefs tucked under arches. And the gilding...wow. The warmth of the gold seems to create such a wonderful light that the windows seem unnecessary. And I totally pixel-peeped. Because it would be a terrible waste to miss any of those bits of hidden treasures if I never get a chance to walk that aisle and make my three wishes in person. Beautiful. And the absolute perfect find. Even better, as it was just you and this glorious space. I was going to make a comment about who needs a stinking tripod when you have a D700, but alas, it appears you brought the 90. So, kudos on quick thinking to use the camera bag! | Shelly MacNeil - Conway: wonderful perspective and beautiful church! | Stella Blu: Wonderful capture !! It looks wonderful without a tripod. I really could not see myself to carry a tripod around in Venice for hours and hours unless I was sure I get to go to a certain place where I need it or would stay there after dark. | Andre Rivest: Beautiful picture Michael, and gracious gesture for your friend who has obviously appreciated. description:
Also known as San Zanipolo, the Italian Gothic San Giovanni is one of the largest churches in Venice yet it has the status of a "minor basilica".  It is the principal Dominican church in Venice. The original church was demolished in 1333 and the current church was completed in 1430. 

The equestrian Monument is of Bartolomeo Colleoni, a professional mercenary.

title: Venezia - Scuola Grande di San Marco, ospedale civile
tags: italy hospital italia marble venezia renaissance architettura confraternita rinascimento marmo ospedale scuolagrandedisanmarco
description:
La Scuola Grande di San Marco si affaccia sul Campo Santi Giovanni e Paolo ed è una delle più significative opere architettoniche del primo Rinascimento veneziano. Al giorno d’oggi costituisce l'ingresso principale dell'ospedale dei santi Giovanni e Paolo. Fu costruita a partire dal 1438 dalla Confraternita di San Marco: infatti le Scuole Grandi nel periodo della Serenissima Repubblica erano associazioni laiche con finalità di mutuo soccorso gestite dalle classi intermedia mercantili che assicuravano agli iscritti soccorso e assistenza in caso di malattia o di problemi economici ma l’attività benefica era rivolta anche alle classi più popolari. Dopo un rovinoso incendio nel 1485 i lavori della ricostruzione furono affidati a Mauro Codussi che realizzò una sede ancora più grande, ricca e fastosa della precedente.
La Scuola fu trasformata in ospedale nel XIX secolo sotto dominazione austriaca.
La facciata, grandiosa combinazione di edicole, lesene corinzie e statue in marmo bianco e policromo è divisa in due parti con struttura asimmetrica.

The Scuola Grande of San Marco, overlooking the Campo of Santi Giovanni e Paolo, is one of the most significant architectural works of the early Venetian Renaissance. Today is the main entrance of the hospital of Santi Giovanni e Paolo. It was built from 1438 by the Confraternity of San Marco: Great Schools in the period of the Serenissima Republic were laic associations for purposes of mutual aid made by the middle merchant classes guaranteed to the members aid and assistance in case of illness or economic problems but the charitable work was also given to the lower classes. After a disastrous fire in 1485 the work of reconstruction was entrusted to Mauro Codussi who built a home even more great, rich and magnificent than the last.
The school was converted into a hospital in the nineteenth century under Austrian rule.
The facade, a great combination of newsagents, Corinthian pilasters and statues in white and polychrome marble is divided into two parts with asymmetric structure.

Classic, architettura classica: Uno scatto per sorprendere la pietra impiegata nel modo più tradizionale nell'architettura, dal classico al moderno Green, architettura bio: Una foto che colga l'utilizzo del materiale lapideo in un contesto di ecosostenibilità, nell'ottica quindi del rispetto dell'ambiente, dell'efficienza energetica e dell'impiego di materiali naturali: un punto di osservazione pensato con la logica della biocompatibilità. La partecipazione è GRATUITA, il PRIMO CLASSIFICATO riceverà un iPad con Wi-Fi 16 GB, il secondo classificato una Canon Power Shot S90. Il concorso si svolge esclusivamente on line, ed è riservato ai maggiori di 18 anni. I partecipanti sono chiamati a inviare le opere fotografiche entro il 10 maggio 2011. per maggiori informazioni www.furrer.it/it/magazine/if-photo-award.asp | Simansi...Preview ( on - off ): QUESTA FOTO E' MOLTO INTERESSANTE!!! description:
La Scuola Grande di San Marco fut construite par le grand Pietro Lombardo dont on peut admirer plusieurs réalisations à Venise. 

C'est actuellement l'hôpital civil de la ville. La Scuola se trouve sur une des plus belles places de La Sérénissime.

Suivant les conseils de Jean Giono qui considérait que le noir était la seule couleur qui apporte quelque chose de nouveau à Venise ; j'ai pensé que l'ensemble rendrait bien traité de cette façon avec la blancheur des marbres des façades de la Scuola.

title: Condottiere
tags: venice italy statue warrior venise venezia venedig renaissance warlord condottiere equestiran andreadelverrocchio
description: 12.4.2010: equestrian statue of the condottiere Bartolomeo Colleoni (1400 - 1475). According to John Julius Norwich, he bequested 216,000 ducats to the Venetian Republic on the condition that an equestrian statue be erected in his memory in St. Mark's square. Since a statue to any single individual (other than a saint) in the piazza would have been too un-republican, this statue was erected outside the scuola (confraternity) of San Marco beside the church of Ss Giovanni e Paolo, Venice.

title: La Scuola Grande di San Marco
tags: italien venice veneza italia itali venise venecia venezia venedig velence itália 2007 italië 威尼斯 venetië venecija venetsia 이탈리아 италия veneetsia olaszország венеция ايطاليا वेनिस इटली ヴェネツィア البندقية ιταλία βενετία veneţia італія венецыя венеція 意大利威尼斯 venesië 베니스의 वेनेज़िया veneciji
description:
La scuola, l'une des six "scuole grande" est en conjonction avec l'église dominicaine Zanipolo (San Giovanni e Paolo) et la statue équestre du condottière Colleoni. L'ensemble architectural dégage un grand charme sur cette place qui s'ouvre sur le rio dei Mendicanti. Le calme règne sur les eaux d'un quartier heureux.
C'est l'occupant autrichien en 1815 qui fit transformer cette confrérie en hôpital qui est toujours en activité.
La Scuola Grande di San Marco est en effet aujourd'hui le siège de l'Hôpital municipal. Il ne faut pas manquer de jeter un oeil à l'entrée et au cloître qui sont étonnants de beauté. La façade polychrome est un des plus beaux appareillages de marbre que l'on puisse admirer à Venise. L'hôpital contient les plus beaux salons de la ville.

title: bellezza tanto antica e tanto nuova / ancient both beauty and so new
tags: italy nikon italia chiesa venezia riflessi controluce bellezza gotico religione spirito sagostino sangiovanniepaolo confessioni conversione coolpixl10
description: Tardi ti ho amato, bellezza tanto antica e tanto nuova, tardi ti ho amato. Ed ecco che tu stavi dentro di me e io ero fuori e là ti cercavo. E io, brutto, mi avventavo sulle cose belle da te create. Eri con me ed io non ero con te. Mi tenevano lontano da te quelle creature che, se non fossero in te, neppure esisterebbero. Mi hai chiamato, hai gridato, hai infranto la mia sordità. Mi hai abbagliato, mi hai folgorato, e hai finalmente guarito la mia cecità. Hai alitato su di me e ora anelo a te. Ti ho gustato e ora ho fame e sete di te. Mi hai toccato e ora ardo dal desiderio di conseguire la tua pace. S. Agostino: Confessioni 10, 27,38 S. Agostino (354-430) Nasce pagano (come tutti noi, del resto), studia e si impadronisce della cultura letteraria e filosofica del suo tempo. Diventa cristiano dopo averne fatte di tutti i colori e, infine, anche prete e vescovo. Se ti va, guardala ingrandita Venezia: Chiesa SS.Giovanni Paolo

Invitation to View my Explore Collection. ] | luciapolofriz: che imponeneti quelle colonne!!! bellissima davvero :-) | francesca!!: mi piace proprio tutto: la composizione, la persona, la luce, la scelta del testo.. | SerenaK. (in stand by): Belle parole che toccano dentro... come la tua bella immagine!!!! | sandroo: Splendida! Hai catturato un'atmosfera veramente magica! | Michele & Miriam Photography: Per le parole dovete ringraziare S.Agostino !!! Per l'atmosfera e la luce: Venezia al confine tra estate e autunno. Vi ringrazio per i commenti. | savory scarf: che bella atmosfera! | Guido Mastrobuono (di Progettocicero): Ciao, i fraticelli francescani del Santuario di Santa Maria delle Grazie (ponticelli) ci hanno chiesto di creare loro un sito che permetta una più efficiente opera di evangelizzazione. Da buoni francescani disponevano di un budget faraonico di 0€. Grazie al servizio volontario di alcuni amici abbiamo creato un sito abbastanza carino all’indirizzo www.santuariodisantamariadellegrazie.org/ ed ora servono immagini per illustrarlo. I temi francescani sono la pace, la conoscenza, l’amore, la fratellanza, la natura, la famiglia ed il bene comune. Insomma, foto che descrivano la vita! Se lo vorrai, ci aiuterebbe moltissimo se tu volessi iscriverti al gruppo www.flickr.com/groups/santuariodisantamariadellegrazie/ e volessi pubblicare nel pool queste ed altre foto in modo da permetterci di inserire le stesse negli articoli. I donatori saranno ringraziati opportunamente. Pace e bene Guido

title: Santi Giovanni e Paolo
description: This huge church contains monuments to a large number of Doges. It adjoins the Scuola Grande di San Marco. The statue is of Colleoni. Colleoni was a mercenary who left his wealth to the Republic on condition that a monument be erected to his memory outside the Basilica di San Marco. The city fathers, to whom the cult of anonymity was everything, took the money but instead erected the monument outside the Scuola Grande di San Marco, in the Campo Santi Giovanni e Paolo

blick in das mittelschiff nach westen.
ich habe ein querformat gewählt, wodurch man auch einen schönen blick in die seitenschiffe gewinnt.

beeindruckend sind die vielen verstrebungen mit eichenbalken. im hauptschiff direkt über den pfeilern in alle vier richtungen und dann nochmal unter dem gewölbeansatz.ob diese vielen inneren verstrebungen auf grund des unsicheren baugrundes oder wegen der breite der schiffe erforderlich waren, ist mir nicht bekannt. auf jeden fall versuchte man in der gotik solche querversteifungen, wenn möglich zu vermeiden.

title: Scuola Grande di San Marco
tags: venice italy panorama architecture hospital photo san europe stitch image stock di unknown marco venezia scuola wwwmattfieldcom
description: "The Scuola Grande di San Marco was one of the six grand scuole or confraternities that had an important presence in Venetian religious and cultural life. Founded in 1260 for religious and humanitarian purposes, it was favored by the Signoria and included in its membership men of great renown and wealth. When Napoleon disbanded the scuole in the early 19C, the building was transformed into Venice's principal hospital. In 1488, the confraternity commissioned Pietro Lombardo to design a façade to replace the previous Gothic building that had burned. The façade is divided into two parts, each with its own entrance portal. Flanking the right portal, are two beautifully sculpted reliefs, attributed to Tullio Lombardo, of scenes from the life of St. Mark. When the Scuola Grande di San Marco was half finished, perhaps due to pressure exerted by certain more influential brethren, the Scuola summoned Pietro Lombardo's principal competitor, architect Mauro Codussi, to complete the project. Codussi finished the upper zone of the fac¸ade around 1495. The Lombardo workshop, although no longer in charge of the project, continued to supply Codussi with sculptures and ornamental elements. The Scuola Grande di San Marco was suppressed by Napoleonic decree after the fall of the Venetian Republic in 1797. The Scuola, along with the adjacent Hospital of San Lazzaro dei Mendicanti and the Dominican convent of SS. Giovanni e Paolo, was transformed into a military hospital in 1808. By 1819 it had become the civic hospital of Venice, as it remains today." (from www.savevenice.org/site/pp.asp?c=9eIHKWMHF&b=67644) Venice, Italy 3 stitched images
comments: loukomades: It is not a church. This building was originally built to house the Scuola di San Marco a confraternity. It is now used as a hospital. | Matthew Field: good to know, thanks

title: Lorenzo Lotto, St. Antoninus Giving Alms, 1541, Venice, Santi Giovanni e Paolo
description:
Painter: Lorenzo Lotto (Venice 1483?-1557)
Date: 1541-42
Original Location: in situ, Santi Giovanni e Paolo, Venice
Medium and Size: canvas
332 x 235 cm
Literature: John Mills in King and Sylvester 1983, p. 16; Humfrey 1997, p. 138; Mack 2002, p. 88

Notes: John Mills reminds us that the painting is called 'Sant' Antonino Elemosinario' or 'St. Antoninus Giving Alms'. 'Rosamond Mack correctly notes that ' The two carpets play a pointed role in the theme: the rich furnishings underline the wealth of the Church as alms are dispensed to the poor,' but in my interpretation, the richness is not the point -- the giving of alms was a sacrament of the Latin church (see 7 Sacraments set) and depictions of alms-giving including oriental carpets as holy ground begin in the 14th c. (see the essay on Circa 1440)

title: San Zanipolo
tags: old city venice urban italy building heritage history church architecture geotagged religious italian europe italia european basilica religion gothic historic chiesa venetian venezia castello europeanunion gothicarchitecture veneto serenissima historicalplaces veneziani sangiovanniepaolo saintjohnandsaintpaul giovanniepaolo sanzanipolo basilicasangiovanniepaolo bartolomeobon geo:lat=45439327 geo:lon=12341638
comments: richardr: The Basilica di San Giovanni e Paolo, known in the Venetian form of San Zanipolo, is one of the largest churches of Venice and has the status of a minor basilica. A huge Gothic brick edifice, it is the Dominican church of Venice, and as such was built for preaching to large congregations. It is dedicated to John and Paul, not the Biblical apostles of the same names, but two obscure martyrs of the Early Christian church in Rome, whose names were recorded in the 3rd century but whose legend is of a later date. After the 15th century the funeral services of all of Venice's doges were held in San Zanipolo. Twenty-five doges are buried in the church, making it something of a Venetian Pantheon. San Zanipolo is a parish church of the Vicariate of San Marco-Castello. In 1246, Doge Jacopo Tiepolo donated some swampland to the Dominicans after dreaming of a flock of white doves flying over it. The first church was demolished in 1333, when the current church was begun. It was not completed until 1430. The West front’s huge unfinished brick façade contrasts with the elegance of the decorated façade of the adjoining Scuola Grande di San Marco. The gateway is by Bartolomeo Bon, with columns salvaged from a church on Torcello, and mixes classical details into its essentially gothic form. It was to be part of a remodelled façade, but the rest never happened. en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basilica_di_San_Zanipolo | ribizlifozelek: Unfinished but beautiful. (1-2-3 History) | richardr: Indeed. | Blunder: Interesting stuff about that dream and the year of desruction; 1333, talk about a bad luck sign. 123H | richardr: Oh, nearly every church in Venice was built because someone or other had a vision of some saint or other on a patch of swampy ground no-one would ever have touched with a bargepole otherwise. Whether the people who had the visions were genuine or not, or whether they simply wanted a crafty way to get a church built, history does not record! On a similar note, the Aztec capital, Tenochitlan, was founded in exactly the same way, after a vision of an eagle settling on a cactus. Presumably they had problems with working out why exactly they'd built their city on a swamp as well. Normally, you just get some legend about Aeneas or Romulus and Remus... | londonconstant: Hello Richardi, Back to brick and stone... I'm an admin for a group called Brick & Stone Architecture, and we'd love to have your photo added to the group.

title: Scuola Grande di San Marco
tags: old city venice urban italy building heritage history architecture facade hospital geotagged italian europe italia european historic venetian marble venezia castello europeanunion scuola serenissima historicalplaces veneziani pietrolombardo codussi scuolagrandedisanmarco scuolasanmarco tulliolombardo maurocodussi opsedale giovannidiantoniobuora geo:lat=45439568 geo:lon=1234138 camposantigiovanniepaolo lomardo
comments: richardr: The Scuola Grande di San Marco was one of the six grand scuole or confraternities that had an important presence in Venetian religious and cultural life. Founded in 1260 for religious and humanitarian purposes, it was favored by the Signoria and included in its membership men of great renown and wealth. When Napoleon disbanded the scuole in the early 19C, the building was transformed into Venice's principal hospital. In 1488, the confraternity commissioned Pietro Lombardo to design a facade to replace the previous Gothic building that had burned. The façade is divided into two parts, each with its own entrance portal. Flanking the right portal, are two beautifully sculpted reliefs, attributed to Tullio Lombardo, of scenes from the life of St. Mark. When the Scuola Grande di San Marco was half finished, perhaps due to pressure exerted by certain more influential brethren, the Scuola summoned Pietro Lombardo's principal competitor, architect Mauro Codussi, to complete the project. Codussi finished the upper zone of the fac¸ade around 1495. The Lombardo workshop, although no longer in charge of the project, continued to supply Codussi with sculptures and ornamental elements. The Scuola Grande di San Marco was suppressed by Napoleonic decree after the fall of the Venetian Republic in 1797. The Scuola, along with the adjacent Hospital of San Lazzaro dei Mendicanti and the Dominican convent of SS. Giovanni e Paolo, was transformed into a military hospital in 1808. By 1819 it had become the civic hospital of Venice, as it remains today. www.savevenice.org/site/pp.asp?c=9eIHKWMHF&b=67644 | ribizlifozelek: Beautiful, well captured facade.
